texto: Throughout the shadowy world of ghosts and demons there is no figure so terrible, no figure so dreaded and abhorred, yet dight with such fearful fascination, as the vampire, who is himself neither ghost nor demon, but yet who partakes the dark natures and and possesses the mysterious and terrible qualities of both.
explicacao: Citação de Montague Summers sobre enfrentar incertezas com ação. “Throughout the shadowy world of ghosts and demons there is no figure so terrible, no figure so dr…” articula reflexao, vampires, figure em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como reflexao-noturna e reflexao.

frase_original: Throughout the shadowy world of ghosts and demons there is no figure so terrible, no figure so dreaded and abhorred, yet dight with such fearful fascination, as the vampire, who is himself neither ghost nor demon, but yet who partakes the dark natures and and possesses the mysterious and terrible qualities of both.
